meta.name: Chicken Biryani
meta.subtitle: Layered basmati rice and spiced chicken cooked dum-style for fragrance and depth

source.name: SSA canonical cuisines/indian/recipes/biryani_chicken.json
notes: Deterministic, home-kitchen biryani: parboiled basmati, marinated chicken, onion-ghee base, dum finish.
Designed to be robust without specialty equipment; supports oven-dum or stovetop-dum.

summary: Fragrant layered biryani with yogurt-marinated chicken, caramelized onions, whole spices, and parboiled basmati rice finished dum-style until tender and aromatic.

yield.notes: Serves 6 as a main; 8 with raita + salad.

notes: Contains dairy (yogurt/ghee). Make dairy-free with coconut yogurt + neutral oil.

optional.label: Electric pressure cooker
optional.notes: Not recommended for classic biryani texture; can be used with caution (risk of mushy rice).

ingredients.label: Chicken thighs (bone-in, skinless preferred)
notes: Bone-in thighs stay juicy and flavor the rice.
Boneless thighs also work; reduce cook time slightly.

ingredients.label: Plain yogurt
notes: Use full-fat for stability; whisk smooth.

ingredients.label: Kashmiri chili powder (or mild chili powder)
notes: For more heat, add 1/2 tsp cayenne.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Split across marinade, rice water, and finishing adjustments.

ingredients.label: Basmati rice, rinsed until water runs mostly clear
notes: Soak 20–30 minutes for longer grains; drain well before parboiling.

ingredients.label: Green cardamom pods
notes: Lightly crush to open pods.

ingredients.label: Yellow onions, thinly sliced
notes: Caramelized onions are key; slice evenly for consistent browning.

ingredients.label: Ghee
notes: Sub neutral oil to reduce dairy.

ingredients.label: Diced tomatoes
notes: Optional: adds body; omit for a drier, onion-forward base.

ingredients.label: Fresh mint leaves, chopped
notes: Strongly recommended for classic aroma.

ingredients.label: Saffron threads
notes: Optional but adds signature biryani perfume and color.

ingredients.label: Warm milk (for saffron bloom)
notes: Use water for dairy-free.

ingredients.label: Water (for rice parboil + dum steam)
notes: Used mostly for boiling rice. You’ll only add a small amount to the dum pot.

steps.title: Marinate the chicken
instructions: In a bowl, whisk yogurt, ginger, garlic, turmeric, cumin, coriander, Kashmiri chili, lemon juice, and 1 tsp salt.
Add chicken and coat thoroughly.
Cover and marinate at least 30 minutes (or up to overnight in the fridge).
checks: If marinating overnight, reduce salt slightly if you’re salt-sensitive.

steps.title: Bloom saffron (optional)
instructions: If using saffron, warm milk (or water) until just hot, not boiling.
Crush saffron threads between fingers and steep in the warm liquid 10 minutes.
checks: Liquid should turn golden and fragrant.

steps.title: Caramelize onions
instructions: Heat ghee in a heavy pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add sliced onions and cook 12–18 minutes, stirring frequently, until deep golden-brown.
Remove about 1/3 of the fried onions to a plate for layering later.
checks: Golden-brown is ideal; avoid burning (bitter). Reduce heat if onions darken too fast.

steps.title: Cook the chicken masala base
instructions: To the remaining onions in the pot, add green chilies (optional) and cook 30 seconds.
Add marinated chicken and scrape in all marinade.
Cook 6–8 minutes, turning pieces once, until chicken is opaque on the outside and yogurt sauce thickens.
If using tomatoes, add them and cook 3 minutes to soften.
Stir in 1 tsp garam masala and half the cilantro + half the mint. Turn off heat.
checks: You are not fully cooking chicken yet; it finishes during dum.
Sauce should look glossy and clingy, not watery.

steps.title: Soak and parboil basmati
instructions: Rinse basmati rice until water runs mostly clear. Soak 20–30 minutes, then drain well.
Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Add 1.5 tsp salt and whole spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaves, peppercorns).
Add drained rice and boil 5–7 minutes until rice is about 70% cooked (still firm in the center).
Drain immediately and discard whole spices if you prefer (or leave a few for aroma).
checks: Key texture: grain should break but show a small opaque core.
Overboiled rice will become mushy during dum.

steps.title: Layer chicken and rice
instructions: Return the pot with chicken masala to low heat.
Spread chicken in an even layer at the bottom.
Add a layer of parboiled rice on top (do not mix).
Scatter reserved fried onions, remaining cilantro/mint, and remaining garam masala over the rice.
Drizzle saffron milk (optional) over the top in streaks.
checks: Do not stir—biryani relies on distinct layers.
If your chicken base looks dry, add 2–3 tbsp water before layering.

steps.title: Dum-cook until done
instructions: Seal the pot: place a tight lid on top. If your lid is loose, cover the pot with foil first, then lid.
Cook on very low heat 25–35 minutes until chicken is cooked through and rice is tender.
Rest 10 minutes off heat before opening.
checks: If stovetop is unreliable, dum in oven at 350°F for 25–30 minutes (lid on).
Chicken doneness: juices run clear; internal temp ~165°F at thickest point.

steps.title: Fluff and serve
instructions: Gently fluff the rice from the top with a fork, lifting some chicken from the bottom without smashing grains.
Taste and adjust salt if needed.
Serve with raita or sliced cucumbers, and a squeeze of lemon if desired.
checks: Use a light hand—overmixing breaks grains and muddies layers.

notes: A cooling side (raita/cucumber) balances spice and richness.
Great for gatherings; flavors deepen after resting.
Cool quickly and refrigerate.
Reheat covered with a splash of water to re-steam grains.
Freeze in portions. Thaw overnight in fridge.
Reheat covered to prevent drying.
Chicken must reach a safe internal temperature before serving.
Do not leave cooked biryani at room temperature more than 2 hours.
After layering, cover pot and bake at 350°F for 25–30 minutes.
Rest 10 minutes before opening.
Use coconut yogurt for marinade and neutral oil instead of ghee.
Bloom saffron in warm water.
Add 1 tsp black pepper and 1 tsp extra Kashmiri chili, plus 1/2 tsp cayenne.
Add 2 tbsp fried onions into the chicken base before layering.
Best as a weekend/project meal; excellent leftovers.
Pairs well with a cooling yogurt side and a crisp salad.
